name: Grimwen Hearth
description: A crossroads town built at the intersection of two major trade routes, with inns and stables on every corner. The old quarter, a preserved medieval warren of narrow alleys and timber-framed buildings. The town's famous bell tower, whose bells ring the hours, call worshippers to prayer, and warn of danger.
atmosphere: Street preachers and political agitators compete for audiences on the town's many corners.
government: A merchant oligarchy governs through a council of the ten wealthiest citizens.

services.name: Crucible & Nail
services.description: A dwarven blacksmith operates this forge. Their metalwork is renowned for its durability.

services.name: Wren's Fabrics
services.description: A small clothing shop with ready-made items in common sizes.

services.name: The Frost Scroll
services.description: A lively tavern with entertainment nightly. Minstrels, storytellers, and the occasional brawler.

notableNPCs.name: Greeba
notableNPCs.description: The Forge-master of Crucible & Nail. Talkative and friendly, eager to share local gossip.

notableNPCs.name: Rangrim
notableNPCs.description: The Clothier of Wren's Fabrics. Eccentric but knowledgeable, prone to rambling stories.

notableNPCs.name: Aelara
notableNPCs.description: The Publican of The Frost Scroll. Sharp-eyed and calculating, always looking for an angle.

rumors: The town's oldest tree split down the middle during a windless night.
A sigil carved into the town square glows with imprisoned magic on certain nights.
Someone carved a warning into the door of the town hall overnight.
threat: The town's isolation has bred suspicion of outsiders, making trade and diplomacy nearly impossible.
